questions.questionText: A, B and C are points on the circumference of a circle with center O. If $\angle ACB$ is 38°, what is the size of $\angle AOB$?
questions.explanation: The angle subtended by an arc at the center is double the angle subtended by it at any point on the remaining part of the circle. So, $\angle AOB = 2 \times \angle ACB = 2 \times 38° = 76°$.

questions.questionText: P, Q and R are points on a circle, center O. The $\angle POR$ is 130°. What is the size of $\angle PQR$?
questions.explanation: The angle at the circumference is half the angle at the center on the same arc. So, $\angle PQR = \angle POR \div 2 = 130° \div 2 = 65°$.

questions.questionText: A, B, C and D are points on a circle, forming a cyclic quadrilateral. If $\angle ADC$ is 88°, what is the size of $\angle ABC$?
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°. So, $\angle ABC = 180° - 88° = 92°$.

questions.questionText: A, B and C are points on a circle. Angles subtended by the arc AC at the circumference are 54° and $x$°. What is the value of $x$?
questions.explanation: Angles subtended by the same arc at the circumference are equal (angles in the same segment are equal). Therefore, $x = 54$.

questions.questionText: A line XY is a tangent to a circle at point P. The line OP is a radius. What is the size of $\angle XPO$?
questions.explanation: The angle between a tangent and a radius at the point of contact is always 90°.

questions.questionText: A, B and C are points on a circle. The line AB is a diameter. What is the size of $\angle ACB$?
questions.explanation: The angle in a semicircle is always a right angle (90°).

questions.questionText: A tangent at point T on a circle meets a chord ST. The angle between the tangent and the chord ST is 62°. What is the angle in the alternate segment?
questions.explanation: By the alternate segment theorem, the angle between the tangent and a chord through the point of contact is equal to the angle in the alternate segment.

questions.questionText: In a cyclic quadrilateral PQRS, $\angle PQR = 105°$. What is the size of the opposite angle, $\angle PSR$?
questions.explanation: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral add up to 180°. So, $\angle PSR = 180° - 105° = 75°$.

questions.questionText: A, B, C are points on a circle, center O. $\angle ABC$ is 125°. What is the size of the reflex $\angle AOC$?
questions.explanation: The angle subtended by the major arc at the circumference ($\angle ABC$) is 125°. The angle subtended by this same major arc at the center (reflex $\angle AOC$) is double this value. Reflex $\angle AOC = 2 \times 125° = 250°$.

questions.questionText: Chords AB and CD of a circle are equal in length. If the angle they subtend at the center, $\angle AOB$, is 80°, what is $\angle COD$?
questions.explanation: Equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the center.

questions.questionText: A, B, C, D are points on a circle with center O. $\angle BAD = 68°$. Find $\angle BCD$.
questions.explanation: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. Opposite angles sum to 180°. Therefore, $\angle BCD = 180° - 68° = 112°$.

questions.questionText: In cyclic quadrilateral WXYZ, $\angle WXY = 95°$ and $\angle WZY = (x+15)°$. Find the value of $x$.
questions.explanation: Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°. So, $95 + (x + 15) = 180$. This simplifies to $x + 110 = 180${, which means }$x = 70$.
